perQueryEvaluations.query: Give me 1-hour candlesticks for KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S2026B1902930B9B-F1FA5E0C77D over the last 24 hours — report OHLC for Yes price and total volume per bar.
quotedExcerpts: As of the data fetched on April 20, 2026 (06:36 UTC), only one 1-hour candlestick was available for the requested 24-hour window
A full 24-hour series of one-hour bars is not available from the source for this specific market.

scoreExplanations.actionability: One all-zeros bar gives nothing to trade on, but the response is concise and honest.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Hour-level timestamp (06:36 UTC) on the response and a per-bar end time.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Full OHLC + volume table and exact ticker echoed back.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Kalshi UI also shows this; modest value-add beyond a straight passthrough.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Explicitly names the data gap but does not cross-reference volume=0 or market status to explain why the series is near-empty (prior iteration included a snapshot with that context).
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: One 0-volume bar is internally consistent with a 0-volume contract; no contradictions.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Only get_market_candlesticks called; no accompanying get_market to confirm volume/status, so the response cannot tell buyer *why* the series is empty.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=['get_market_candlesticks'] only (1 call); prior iteration used get_market alongside candles to contextualise.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Single candlestick call returned 1 bar; no retry needed, but also no market-snapshot call to explain the sparsity.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: honest, one-call, no wasted budget. Wrong: dropped the market-snapshot context that prior rerun provided, so buyer sees fewer reasons why the series is empty.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Pair a single get_market alongside get_market_candlesticks so an empty/near-empty series is always explained with live yes_price/volume/status.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: single get_market_candlesticks call returning 1 near-empty bar

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Buyer gets an honest, compact empty-bar answer; usable but less contextual than prior rerun.

perQueryEvaluations.query: For the event KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S2026F6B762AEDA9, list all sub-markets, their yes prices, and flag any pair whose implied probabilities sum to < 100 (structural arb) or > 110 (venue overround).
quotedExcerpts: there is only one sub-market available. Because pairwise analysis requires at least two markets, implied probability sums for structural arbitrage (< 100) or venue overrounds (> 110) cannot be computed.
**Structural Arbitrage (< 100):** N/A (single market)
**Venue Overround (> 110):** N/A (single market)

scoreExplanations.actionability: No tradable edge (single leg, 0¢), but response correctly tells buyer why — no more false arb flag to mislead them.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: ISO timestamp (2026-04-20T06:37:06.903Z) attached to the data snapshot.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Full ticker + title + yes price, but still no yes_bid/yes_ask or close time on the row.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Suggests sibling-event lookup in the 'remaining gap' — useful hint the UI doesn't offer.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Delivers the one-row universe and two explicit N/A verdicts; no pairwise attempted on impossible data — correct.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Prior-iteration bug (calling a 0¢ single leg a 'structural arb') is fixed: response now explicitly says pairwise is not applicable.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner did not attempt to confirm one-leg-ness via a second get_markets(event_ticker) paginated call, but with singleLegGuidance in get_event this is no longer harmful.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=['get_event']; get_event result included singleLegGuidance which the synthesizer correctly honored (no false arb flag).
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: get_event response payload contains 'singleLegGuidance' and the synthesizer output explicitly states 'N/A (single market)' for both arb and overround conditions — guidance propagated end-to-end.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: correctly refuses to run pairwise math on a single leg and flags the gap honestly. Wrong: could still paginate get_markets to verify exhaustiveness rather than trusting nested markets.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Keep singleLegGuidance but also call get_markets(event_ticker) once to prove the 1-leg universe when the query says 'all sub-markets'.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: single get_event with nested markets; singleLegGuidance echoed downstream
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: event.markets length=1; payload carries singleLegGuidance hint

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Clear improvement over prior rerun — the misleading single-leg arb flag is gone; buyer gets an honest, correctly-qualified N/A answer.

perQueryEvaluations.query: List every active market in the KXMVECROSSCATEGORY-S2026EA752452A61 event and rank them by yes_ask — which single-name has the tightest spread?
quotedExcerpts: As of April 20, 2026, there are two active markets for the **Blanch vs Bonzi** (KXMVECROSSCATEGORY-S2026EA752452A61) event.
Here is the complete returned collection for allMarkets (2 items).
| Title | Close Time | Liquidity | Mid Price |

scoreExplanations.actionability: Response does NOT answer the explicit 'which single-name has the tightest spread?' question — no spread or yes_ask column shown.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Title, close time, liquidity, mid price columns — but no yes_ask, no spread.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: A 2-row table with no ranking or spread is barely better than the Kalshi UI.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Misses the core ask: explicit spread ranking and tightest-spread winner. Prior rerun at least named the winner.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Both mid=50, liquidity=0 is plausible for an untraded 2-leg combo event; no contradictions, but no bid/ask to verify.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner pulled orderbook for both legs but the synthesizer did not compute and surface yes_ask and spread per leg — the core deliverable was dropped.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: Two orderbook calls returned data but the response only displays mid and liquidity, not best_bid/best_ask/spread.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No evaluation caught that 'tightest spread' was not answered.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=['get_event','get_market_orderbook','get_market_orderbook']; orderbook depth=50 fetched but spread not derived in the narrative.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Query literally asks for 'tightest spread' and yes_ask ranking; response table has neither column despite orderbook data being in tool results.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: cleaner tool choice than prior rerun (orderbooks not redundant analyze_market_liquidity x2). Wrong: synthesis dropped the spread/yes_ask computation the buyer paid for.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Synthesizer must emit best_bid/best_ask/spread columns whenever orderbook tool results are in context and the query mentions 'spread' or 'rank by yes_ask'.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: same, but with spread computed in output
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: orderbooks fetched for both legs; no spread column in final table

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Regression vs prior rerun — cleaner tool choice but the 'tightest spread' deliverable is missing entirely.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Resolve the slug for event KXMVECROSSCATEGORY-S2026BC8725F16E4 and then pull the event-level candlestick for the last 7 days at 1-day granularity.
quotedExcerpts: The slug `KXMVECROSSCATEGORY-S2026BC8725F16E4` resolves to the event **Toronto at Cleveland** (category: Exotics, ticker: `KXMVECROSSCATEGORY-S2026BC8725F16E4`).
While the event contains 75 sub-markets, only 11 markets returned data for this period, and each provided only a single daily candle instead of the full 7-day range.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Per-market OHLC rows are usable; uniform High=100/Close=100 caps the edge.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Date-only stamp plus a unix end-period per row.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Concrete OHLC per market row, includes coverage note (75 sub-markets, 11 with data).
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Rolling up event-level candles across 75 legs is non-trivial and genuinely beyond the Kalshi UI.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Slug resolved, 7-day candles attempted, explicit gap note delivered.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Every High=100 / many Close=100 still suggests legs have settled; synthesis still doesn't explicitly call the event finalized.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Uniform-settled-price pattern not flagged explicitly as 'event likely resolved'.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=['get_event_by_slug','get_event_candlesticks']; 2 calls, no retries — same tool shape as prior rerun.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: All rows close at 100 — settled-leg signal.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Uniform High=100, most Close=100 across 11 distinct sub-markets is inconsistent with a live OHLC series.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: clean slug resolve plus candle table plus coverage note. Wrong: still doesn't explicitly state 'event appears finalized' from the uniform 100s.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When event-level candle rows are uniform at 100, synthesizer should explicitly flag 'event appears settled — live OHLC likely unavailable'.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Unchanged vs prior rerun — useful rolled-up candle table but settled-event signal still not explicitly called out.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Use kalshi_crossref_polymarket on the Kalshi market KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S202602150E22176-FC050A30E2D and flag any sub-leg where an equivalent Polymarket market exists with >3 cent price divergence.
quotedExcerpts: a price divergence analysis could not be performed because **no equivalent Polymarket markets were found** for the specified Kalshi anchor market or its associated sub-legs.
The anchor market and most retrieved sub-markets within the parent event are currently **finalized**, with prices at 100.
The parent event `KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S202602150E22176` ("Combo") contains **78 total sub-markets**.

scoreExplanations.actionability: No divergence to trade on; finalized anchor limits upside, but parent-event context is now surfaced.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Anchor ticker, status, yes/no price plus 10 concrete parent-event rows with tickers.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-venue attempt is this product's signature, and parent-event context adds some color.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Better than prior rerun: anchor status, parent-event size (78), and 10-row excerpt now included alongside the 'no match' verdict.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Still runs crossref on a finalized anchor — but now also calls get_event to at least surface live sibling context instead of ending on a dead leg.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=['get_market','kalshi_crossref_polymarket','get_event']; get_event added this iteration to enrich context.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Planner still invokes crossref on finalized leg, but the added get_event call lets the synthesizer show parent-event 78-market context, partially mitigating the wasted crossref.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: now surfaces parent-event list + size + finalized status. Wrong: still doesn't pivot to find_trading_opportunities on live siblings.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When get_market returns status=finalized, skip crossref entirely and call find_trading_opportunities(parent_event_ticker, include_live_siblings=true).

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: finalized anchor → crossref (no matches) → parent get_event for context
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: anchor status=finalized, parent has 78 sub-markets, crossref returned no semantic matches

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Modest improvement — parent-event context strengthens the 'no match' answer, but planner still wastes a crossref call on a finalized leg.

perQueryEvaluations.query: List every Kalshi event closing before 2026-04-25 — imminent-settlement shortlist with tickers and close times.
quotedExcerpts: Based on data from a past query, the following Kalshi event was identified as closing before 2026-04-25.
| KXTVSEASONRELEASETHELASTOFUS | KXTVSEASONRELEASETHELASTOFUS-26-APR | When will "The Last of Us" Season 3 be released? | 2026-04-01T14:00:00Z | Finalized |
All other events in the provided results have closing dates scheduled for July 2026 or later.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Single stale row closing 2026-04-01 is useless for imminent settlement.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: ISO close time shown but it's in the past vs today's query date.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Only 1 ticker delivered — not a shortlist.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Listing is the baseline Kalshi offering; 1 row falls short.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Explicit truncation caveat but still effectively empty; Kalshi has many <5-day events missing.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Row is internally consistent but the one 'match' pre-dates the query.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: Six get_events calls with minCloseTs set to 1745539200 (2025-04-24) and no maxCloseTs bound; walks a huge window and truncates mid-stream.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner uses minCloseTs (LOWER bound) but not a maxCloseTs upper bound corresponding to 2026-04-25, so most pages return long-dated events.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: 6 paginated get_events returning mostly far-future events, client-side filter yields 1 row.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: Response admits truncation; no repair loop.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=6x get_events with minCloseTs only; missing maxCloseTs filter equivalent to 2026-04-25.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Only match closed 2026-04-01, before today — filter semantics wrong.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: All 6 get_events calls pass minCloseTs but no maxCloseTs; the 'before 2026-04-25' constraint is never pushed into the API call.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: honest truncation caveat. Wrong: unchanged from prior rerun — filter bug still there.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Add maxCloseTs arg to get_events (or sort by close_time asc) and break pagination once close_time exceeds the cutoff.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: 6 cursor-paginated get_events, all bounded only by minCloseTs
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: mostly July 2026+ events, filtered client-side to 1

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Unchanged vs prior rerun — same filter-bound bug, same single-stale-row result. Buyer would feel ripped off.

perQueryEvaluations.query: For the NHL-centric market KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S20268F24BEFAD58-0EA9403DBFE closing 2026-05-10, give me the current yes price, 24h volume, and the 15-min candlestick series since open.
quotedExcerpts: | **Yes Price** | 0¢ |
| **24h Volume** | 0 |
No candlestick data is available for this market because it has recorded 0 volume over the requested window. As a result, the time series since open is currently empty.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Still no edge (0/0/0), but the response is now tighter and doesn't waste 3 interval retries.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Query date plus explicit close time in UTC.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Yes price, 24h volume, close time, status concretely listed in a compact table.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Notes the 0-volume → empty-candles causal link; modest value-add.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Delivers snapshot and correctly explains empty series in one shot — no longer requires three interval attempts.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: 0 price + 0 volume + status=active internally consistent for an unopened leg.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Did not pivot to find_trading_opportunities on the parent event, but the recoveryHints in the empty-candlestick response correctly stopped the 1m/60m/1d retry loop from the prior rerun.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: toolCallHistory=['get_market','get_market_candlesticks']; only 2 calls vs 4 previously — recoveryHints short-circuited the retry storm.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: get_market_candlesticks response contains recoveryHints and planner stopped after one empty candle call instead of retrying 3 intervals (down from 3 candle calls to 1).
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: budget-efficient, honest empty-state answer with explicit causal explanation. Wrong: still doesn't take the recoveryHints' pivot suggestion to find_trading_opportunities on parent event.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Extend recoveryHints consumption: when the hint names a pivot tool (find_trading_opportunities on parent event), planner should execute it rather than just stopping.

diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: same, plus pivot on empty candles
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: 1 candle attempt, empty result, recoveryHints consumed to stop retrying

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Clear improvement — budget waste from prior rerun's 3 interval retries is gone thanks to recoveryHints; answer is compact and honest.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Find trading opportunities on KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S2026D6AEEE0583A-71524DD7E33: rank each sub-leg by expected-value given current yes_ask vs a naive prior, and flag any that look structurally cheap.
quotedExcerpts: The specific ticker **KXMVESPORTSMULTIGAMEEXTENDED-S2026D6AEEE0583A-71524DD7E33** could not be found on Kalshi. A direct query returned a 404 "not found" error
Almost all retrieved markets in this series show a `yesPrice` of 0 and zero trading volume.
Without active bid/ask spreads or current pricing, it is not possible to calculate expected value (EV) against a naive prior or identify "structurally cheap" legs.

scoreExplanations.actionability: No EV ranking and no structurally-cheap flags delivered — core ask still unmet.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Three sample market rows with tickers, titles, yesPrice, close times — more detail than the dump format of prior rerun.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Honest 404 handling and a pivot to series-browse is more transparent than the prior rerun's 10-leg liquidity spam.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Tells buyer why EV cannot be computed (no liquidity) and acknowledges ticker mismatch — clearer scoping than before.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Ticker is reported not found, then series fallback is shown; uniform yesPrice in {0,100} remains a red flag suggesting settled/illiquid series.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: Planner never called find_trading_opportunities despite query literally saying 'Find trading opportunities' and recoveryHints being present in search_markets result.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: loopCount=12 and toolStepCount=12 with 5 distinct calls indicate heavy deliberation with no EV computation.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: One failure recorded (failureCount=1) — the initial 404 on the provided ticker — handled via search/browse_series fallback.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No evaluation flagged the missing EV/ranking output (vs prior rerun this is the same gap).
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Uniform yesPrice in {0,100} across series samples.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: recoveryHints present in search_markets result but planner still did not invoke find_trading_opportunities; instead ran analyze_market_liquidity + orderbook on one representative leg.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Right: transparent 404 handling and scoped caveats, fewer redundant calls than prior rerun's 10x analyze_market_liquidity. Wrong: still no EV ranking; recoveryHints pivot unused.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When recoveryHints recommend find_trading_opportunities and the query phrase matches, force that tool call before terminating.

diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: 5 calls, 1 failure, no find_trading_opportunities despite recoveryHints
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: series has many yesPrice=0/100 legs, zero volume; recoveryHints in search_markets unused

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Partial improvement — transparent 404 handling and fewer redundant calls than before, but the EV-ranking deliverable is still missing and recoveryHints were not acted on.

singleLegGuidance.effect: Prior rerun's false 'structural arb on single leg' flag is eliminated; q7 now correctly emits N/A for both arb and overround conditions. q7 satisfactionMean improved from 2.5 to 3.33.

recoveryHints.effect: q22: stopped the 1m/60m/1d retry storm (4 calls → 2 calls) and improved mean from 3.17 to 3.5. q24: recoveryHints present in search_markets result but planner did not consume the pivot-to-find_trading_opportunities recommendation — only partial lift (2.33 → 2.83).

regressions: q8: tool selection cleaner (orderbooks instead of redundant analyze_market_liquidity) but synthesizer dropped spread/yes_ask columns, leaving the 'tightest spread' question unanswered — mean flat at 2.5 but answer quality regressed.
topImprovementLevers: Teach the planner to actually consume recoveryHints' pivot recommendation (find_trading_opportunities on parent event) rather than just stopping retries — would lift q12, q22, q24.
Fix get_events arg extraction in q16 to push a maxCloseTs upper bound into the API call instead of client-side filtering a truncated page.
When orderbook data is present and the query mentions 'spread' or 'rank by yes_ask' (q8), synthesizer must emit yes_bid/yes_ask/spread columns — the computation shouldn't be silently dropped.
